item.title: Ba players to receive share of Fiji FACT winners' prize money
item.description: Roylux Realty Ba FC players will be rewarded for their historic BiC Fiji FACT 2026 triumph, with the squad set to share the club's $17,000 winners' prize money and receive an additional bonus. Ba ended a 16-year wait for the Fiji FACT title after defeating IRA Group Navua FC 3-1 in a penalty shootout following a scoreless draw after extra time in the grand final at Subrail Park in Labasa.
item.content:encoded:
Roylux Realty Ba FC players will be rewarded for their historic BiC Fiji FACT 2026 triumph, with the squad set to share the club's $17,000 winners' prize money and receive an additional bonus.

Ba ended a 16-year wait for the Fiji FACT title after defeating IRA Group Navua FC 3-1 in a penalty shootout following a scoreless draw after extra time in the grand final at Subrail Park in Labasa.

Ba Association President Praneel Dayal confirms that the players will receive their share of the prize money, saying they deserve to be recognised for their commitment and outstanding performances throughout the tournament.

Dayal says the players worked extremely hard and fully deserve the reward for bringing the Fiji FACT trophy back to Ba.

He says while celebrations have already begun among the club's supporters, plans for an official victory celebration are yet to be finalised.

He adds that the association will meet soon to discuss its plans, but for now the team's focus has shifted back to the Extra Premier League.

Dayal says he has been overwhelmed by the messages of congratulations and phone calls received from supporters, former players and well-wishers since Ba's triumph.

He thanked everyone for believing in the team and supporting them throughout their journey.

He also acknowledged the efforts of the coaching staff, management, players and executives, saying the title was the result of months of dedication, sacrifice and teamwork.

Dayal says the success belongs to everyone involved with Ba Football and thanked those working behind the scenes for helping make the achievement possible.

He adds that, with the Fiji FACT trophy secured, the Men in Black are determined to maintain their winning form as they continue their campaign in the Extra Premier League, where they will host Nadi FC at 3pm this Sunday.

item.title: Rugby more than a game as Fiji returns to Cardiff for home match on Sunday
item.description: She says she is a proud member of the Fijian community in Wales; and rugby has always been at the heart of that connection. Tukadra says she recalls reading an article that says rugby to Fiji is like football to Brazil, and it brings everyone together, and whenever Fiji plays, they are proud, and when they win, they get emotional.
item.content:encoded:
She says she is a proud member of the Fijian community in Wales; and rugby has always been at the heart of that connection.

Tukadra says she recalls reading an article that says rugby to Fiji is like football to Brazil, and it brings everyone together, and whenever Fiji plays, they are proud, and when they win, they get emotional.

She says she cries because she knows how much it means to the people.

Another fellow Fijian, Sosiceni Tukadra whos father served in the Fiji Royal Military Forces during the Malayan campaign before moving to the United Kingdom in the early 1960s was recruited to help rebuild Britain after the war.

He settled in Newport, where he was later joined by his wife after her long sea voyage from Fiji.

Together they built a new life, raised a family and found a welcoming community in Wales.

Tukadra says the Welsh became like her parents' second family; they welcomed them with open arms, and that sense of belonging is reflected in one of her most treasured family keepsakes — a newspaper clipping from her christening at St David's Church, where she and another child were baptised together in what was described as a lesson in racial integration.

With no relatives nearby, family friends became her godparents, creating bonds that have lasted a lifetime.

The family's love for rugby stretches back to Fiji's first tour of Wales in 1964, and Sosicenis father treasured the official match programme from that historic encounter and often shared stories from the reception after the game.

He says his father used to laugh about how people thought the Fijian players were so religious that everyone would only be drinking soft drinks,and when the Fiji team arrived carrying bottles of whisky and pints of beer.

For Sosiceni, that story perfectly captures the warmth and spirit Fijians bring wherever they go.

In other matches this weekend, New Zealand will play France at 7.10pm Saturday in Christchurch and Australia takes on Ireland at 10.10pm, while South Africa will face England at 3.40am Sunday with Argentina playing Scotland at 7.10am.

item.title: Women’s Super League returns with six-team format as part of Digicel Fiji partnership
item.description: Fiji Football Association Women’s Super League returns with an expanded and more competitive 2026 season as Digicel Fiji continues its long-standing support for women’s football in the country. Digicel Fiji says the country’s top tier of women’s football has been streamlined this year to feature six elite teams, including the addition of Lautoka Women’s Football Club, in a move aimed at strengthen
item.content:encoded:
Fiji Football Association Women’s Super League returns with an expanded and more competitive 2026 season as Digicel Fiji continues its long-standing support for women’s football in the country.

Digicel Fiji says the country’s top tier of women’s football has been streamlined this year to feature six elite teams, including the addition of Lautoka Women’s Football Club, in a move aimed at strengthening competition and improving the pathway to national selection.

They say the league continues to grow in both visibility and standard, with established sides such as Ba Women FC and Labasa Women FC expected to again set the pace in the new season.

Digicel Fiji has reaffirms its commitment as title sponsor, saying its support is focused on developing opportunities for women and young girls in football through its “With You” campaign.

Digicel Fiji CEO Farid Mohammed says football is more than just a sport, describing it as a platform that can change lives and build confidence among young athletes.

He says the company remains committed to supporting players from grassroots level through to elite competition, ensuring they are given opportunities to grow and succeed.

Fiji Football Association President Rajesh Patel says Digicel’s continued support has been instrumental in the development of women’s football in Fiji.

He says the partnership has helped expand opportunities for female players and strengthen development pathways, adding that the league continues to inspire young girls across the country to pursue the sport.

The Women’s Super League, established in 2021, has developed into a key national platform showcasing some of Fiji’s most promising female footballers and remains central to the growth of women’s football in the country.

item.title: Paraguay stun Germany 4-3 on penalties to reach FIFA World Cup last 16
item.description: Paraguay has produced one of the biggest upsets of the FIFA World Cup, knocking four-time champions Germany out of the tournament with a dramatic 4-3 penalty shootout victory after the match finished 1-all following extra time. Julio Enciso gave Paraguay the lead just before halftime with a 42nd-minute strike before Kai Havertz levelled the scores for Germany in the 54th minute.
item.content:encoded:
Paraguay has produced one of the biggest upsets of the FIFA World Cup, knocking four-time champions Germany out of the tournament with a dramatic 4-3 penalty shootout victory after the match finished 1-all following extra time.

Julio Enciso gave Paraguay the lead just before halftime with a 42nd-minute strike before Kai Havertz levelled the scores for Germany in the 54th minute.

Despite dominating possession for long periods and creating several chances, Germany could not find a winner in either normal time or extra time, forcing the match into penalties.

Paraguay held their nerve in the shootout, converting four spot-kicks to seal a famous victory and spark jubilant celebrations from the players and fans.

The result sees Paraguay advance to the Round of 16, while Germany suffers one of the most disappointing exits in the nation's football history.

It is also the first time Germany has lost a penalty shootout at the FIFA World Cup finals, ending a reputation built on decades of shootout success.

Germany was playing in its 21st FIFA World Cup , extending its record as one of the tournament's most successful nations with four World Cup titles in 1954, 1974, 1990 and 2014.

For Paraguay, this is their 9th FIFA World Cup appearance and one of the greatest victories in the country's football history as they progress to the last 16.

Paraguay will now look to continue their dream run when they take on the winner of the France and Sweden Round of 32 clash.

In other matches, Brazil advanced into the last 16 with a dramatic 2-1 victory over Japan.

Meanwhile the Netherlands will take on Morocco at 1pm.

item.title: Martinelli's stoppage-time goal sends Brazil into FIFA World Cup Round of 16
item.description: Gabriel Martinelli struck deep into stoppage time to send Brazil into the FIFA World Cup Round of 16 with a dramatic 2-1 victory o

item.title: Flying Fijians step up preparations ahead of Wales Test
item.description: The FIJI Water Flying Fijians have wasted no time getting to work in Wales, heading straight into their first training session at Cardiff as preparations intensify ahead of their opening Test this weekend. The Fiji Rugby Union confirms that the squad is now in full game-week mode, with two field sessions scheduled as they fine-tune their preparations for the opening clash against Wales. Adding to
item.content:encoded:
The FIJI Water Flying Fijians have wasted no time getting to work in Wales, heading straight into their first training session at Cardiff as preparations intensify ahead of their opening Test this weekend. The Fiji Rugby Union confirms that the squad is now in full game-week mode, with two field sessions scheduled as they fine-tune their preparations for the opening clash against Wales. Adding to the Fijian presence in Wales, local artists Paradise Rootz, Primavia, and Georgina Ledua will perform before each of Fiji's three tour matches.

The Flying Fijians take on Wales this Saturday at 1.10am before facing England on July 11th at Hill Dickinson Stadium. They will then wrap up their tour against Scotland on July 18th at Murrayfield Stadium.

In other matches this weekend, New Zealand will play France at 7.10pm Saturday in Christchurch and Australia takes on Ireland at 10pm, while South Africa will face England at 3.40am Sunday with Argentina playing Scotland at 7.10am.

item.title: Celebrations erupt as Ba FC brings BiC Fiji FACT trophy home
item.description: Ba Football’s long-awaited BiC Fiji FACT triumph after 16 years has been celebrated been with jubilant scenes through the streets of Ba Town. The Men in Black returned home with the coveted trophy after defeating Navua in the final, ending the wait for the prestigious title.
item.content:encoded:
Ba Football’s long-awaited BiC Fiji FACT triumph after 16 years has been celebrated been with jubilant scenes through the streets of Ba Town.

The Men in Black returned home with the coveted trophy after defeating Navua in the final, ending the wait for the prestigious title.

Following their victory, players, officials and supporters marched through Ba Town with the trophy, celebrating the historic achievement with hundreds of fans lining the streets.

The BiC Fiji FACT title is Ba FC's first since 2010, ending a 16-year drought and giving their loyal supporters plenty to celebrate.

item.title: Legge proud as Drua Women book home semi-final match
item.description: That is the confident declaration from the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's coach, Mike Legge after his side produced a dominant 50-17 win over the Reds to secure a place in the semi-finals. Legge says he was pleased with the team's overall effort, noting that they had spent the past two to three weeks working on improving their starts, building pressure and maintaining intensity throughout matches.
item.content:encoded:
That is the confident declaration from the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's coach, Mike Legge after his side produced a dominant 50-17 win over the Reds to secure a place in the semi-finals.

Legge says he was pleased with the team's overall effort, noting that they had spent the past two to three weeks working on improving their starts, building pressure and maintaining intensity throughout matches.

He says those improvements were evident on the field, with the playersÂ largely executing the game plan well.

The coach also praised the team's defensive effort, highlighting improvements in tackle technique and line speed, which he says were key positives from the match.

The Drua Women's semi-final opponents in two weeks are yet to be confirmed.

item.title: Ba edge Navua 3-1 during penalties to win BiC Fiji FACT after 16-year wait
item.description: Ba claim the BiC Fiji FACT title after defeating Navua 3-1 in a dramatic penalty shootout at ending a 16-year wait for a major tit
